description: Measured values, statistics and formulas extracted from Berns et al. (2005), 'Neurobiological Correlates of Social Conformity and Independence During Mental Rotation', expressed as single words combined into triples, plus formulas, sources and values. An fMRI study using a modified Asch paradigm with a mental rotation task.

words.name: conformity
words.description: agreeing with an exogenous source of information when that information is wrong

words.name: independence
words.description: giving the correct answer when the external information presented is incorrect

words.name: social
words.description: relating to interaction with other people, as opposed to interaction with machines or non-human sources

words.name: participant
words.description: a normal adult volunteer who performed the mental rotation task

words.name: actor
words.description: hired confederate who posed as a fellow participant and gave predetermined answers

words.name: error
words.description: an incorrect same/different judgment in the mental rotation task

words.name: rate
words.description: frequency or proportion measured per unit of comparison

words.name: count
words.description: numerical extent or number of a set

words.name: age
words.description: age of a participant in years

words.name: baseline
words.description: trials in which no group or computer information was given

words.name: group
words.description: the social source of external information, i.e. the four peer actors

words.name: computer
words.description: the non-human source of external information

words.name: incorrect
words.description: external information that was wrong

words.name: reaction
words.description: the act of responding to a stimulus

words.name: time
words.description: duration measured for a process

words.name: disparity
words.description: angular difference of orientation between the two rotated objects

words.name: angle
words.description: an angular quantity measured in degrees

words.name: stimulus
words.description: one of the paired three-dimensional Shepard and Metzler objects

words.name: same
words.description: stimulus pairs that are the same object rotated relative to each other

words.name: different
words.description: stimulus pairs that are mirror images rotated relative to each other

words.name: accuracy
words.description: rated or actual proportion of correct judgments

words.name: self
words.description: rating made by the participant about their own performance

words.name: actual
words.description: the true measured performance

words.name: trial
words.description: a single presentation and judgment cycle in the task

words.name: voxel
words.description: a volumetric pixel, the unit of a functional MRI image

words.name: cluster
words.description: a contiguous group of significantly activated voxels

words.name: activation
words.description: increase in blood-oxygenation-level-dependent (BOLD) signal

words.name: threshold
words.description: significance level applied to select activated voxels

words.name: scan
words.description: an individual echo-planar imaging acquisition volume

words.name: kernel
words.description: the gaussian smoothing kernel applied to functional images

words.name: field
words.description: magnetic field strength of the MRI scanner

words.name: statistic
words.description: a value summarising a statistical test (t or F)

words.name: amygdala
words.description: subcortical structure associated with emotional salience

words.name: caudate
words.description: head of the caudate nucleus, part of the striatum

words.name: study
words.description: systematic investigation undertaken to discover facts or reach new conclusions

words.name: neurobiological
words.description: relating to the biology of the nervous system, in particular brain structure and function

words.name: mental
words.description: relating to the mind and cognitive processes

words.name: rotation
words.description: the action of turning an object about an axis

words.name: year
words.description: the orbital period of the Earth around the Sun, the standard unit for dating calendar events
the common-era calendar year 2005

words.name: duration
words.description: a length of time in the experiment

words.name: page
words.description: journal page number

words.name: volume
words.description: journal volume number

words.name: millisecond
words.description: 1/1000 of a second

words.name: millimetre
words.description: 1/1000 of a metre

words.name: mean
words.description: arithmetic mean

words.name: SD
words.description: standard deviation

words.name: SEM
words.description: standard error of the mean

words.name: minimum
words.description: lower bound of a range

words.name: maximum
words.description: upper bound of a range

words.name: x
words.description: x stereotactic coordinate

words.name: y
words.description: y stereotactic coordinate

words.name: z
words.description: z stereotactic coordinate

words.name: lower
words.description: lower value of a stated range

words.name: upper
words.description: upper value of a stated range

words.name: uncorrected
words.description: uncorrected for multiple comparisons

words.name: female
words.description: female participants

words.name: male
words.description: male participants

words.name: excluded
words.description: participants excluded from analysis

words.name: discarded
words.description: participant discarded for imaging artifact

words.name: initial
words.description: value before exclusions

words.name: interaction
words.description: interaction effect in the ANOVA model

words.name: display
words.description: stimulus/response display duration

words.name: reslicing
words.description: spatial normalization reslicing resolution
the natural number one
the natural number two

words.name: F
words.description: Fisher F test statistic

words.name: MNI
words.description: Montreal Neurological Institute stereotactic space

words.name: ROI
words.description: region of interest selected for analysis

words.name: answer
words.description: response given to a stimulus

words.name: both
words.description: two qualifying entities holding simultaneously

words.name: centre
words.description: central point of a region

words.name: coefficient
words.description: a multiplicative factor or constant

words.name: condition
words.description: experimental condition under which a trial was run

words.name: conformed
words.description: agreed with the external information despite being confident

words.name: coordinate
words.description: numerical position in a coordinate system

words.name: correct
words.description: matching the ground truth answer

words.name: correlation
words.description: statistical association between two variables

words.name: decision
words.description: choice made between alternatives

words.name: degree
words.description: unit of angle equal to 1/360 of a full rotation

words.name: diameter
words.description: straight-line distance through the centre of a sphere

words.name: difference
words.description: arithmetic difference between two values

words.name: dimension
words.description: size of one axis of a multi-axis array

words.name: echo
words.description: reflected signal recorded after excitation

words.name: effect
words.description: change attributable to a manipulation

words.name: extent
words.description: spatial reach of a contiguous activation cluster

words.name: freedom
words.description: unconstrained parameter that may vary independently

words.name: gyrus
words.description: rounded ridge on the cerebral cortex

words.name: head
words.description: anterior portion of the caudate nucleus

words.name: hoc
words.description: for the specific purpose described

words.name: information
words.description: message provided to the participant about the correct response

words.name: intercept
words.description: value of the regression line at the origin of the independent variable

words.name: intraparietal
words.description: within the parietal lobe, between the somatosensory and visual areas

words.name: laptop
words.description: portable personal computer

words.name: left
words.description: on the left side of the brain

words.name: limit
words.description: maximum allowable value of a quantity

words.name: main
words.description: primary, isolated from interactions

words.name: majority
words.description: larger fraction of a group

words.name: matrix
words.description: rectangular grid of acquired samples

words.name: no
words.description: negation; indicates the absence of the qualified concept

words.name: occipital
words.description: relating to the occipital lobe of the brain

words.name: origin
words.description: point at which a coordinate axis crosses zero

words.name: p
words.description: probability of obtaining the observed result under the null hypothesis

words.name: per
words.description: partitioning qualifier; once for each instance of the named unit

words.name: phase
words.description: one distinguishable segment of a procedure

words.name: post
words.description: after the primary analysis

words.name: practice
words.description: preparatory exercise before the actual measurement

words.name: publication
words.description: release of a written work

words.name: questionnaire
words.description: set of written questions used to gather responses

words.name: rating
words.description: rated value on a scale, often self-reported

words.name: regression
words.description: fit of a relationship between variables

words.name: repetition
words.description: periodic interval at which an event repeats

words.name: resolution
words.description: smallest distinguishable difference

words.name: response
words.description: reaction emitted by the participant

words.name: right
words.description: on the right side or, in answers, matching the correct option

words.name: round
words.description: one cycle of a repeated procedure

words.name: rt
words.description: reaction time abbreviation

words.name: second
words.description: SI base unit of time

words.name: size
words.description: extent or number of constituents of a set

words.name: slice
words.description: one two-dimensional acquisition plane of a volume

words.name: slope
words.description: rate of change of the regression line

words.name: sphere
words.description: three-dimensional ball-shaped region

words.name: split
words.description: separated into two parts; here describing ambiguous decision trials

words.name: sulcus
words.description: fissure between two brain gyri

words.name: superior
words.description: upper, located dorsally

words.name: sure
words.description: participant is confident in their own answer

words.name: t
words.description: Student t test statistic

words.name: type
words.description: category to which an instance belongs

words.name: unit
words.description: standard quantity used as a measure

words.name: unsure
words.description: participant lacks confidence in their own answer

words.name: value
words.description: a quantitative magnitude assigned to a quantity

triples.name: participant count
triples.description: number of participants retained in the final analysis

triples.name: actor count
triples.description: number of hired actors who played the role of the group

triples.name: mean participant age
triples.description: mean age of the participant sample in years

triples.name: baseline error rate
triples.description: mean error rate on trials with no external information

triples.name: group incorrect error rate
triples.description: mean error rate when the group gave wrong information

triples.name: computer incorrect error rate
triples.description: mean error rate when the computers gave wrong information

triples.name: different minus same reaction time
triples.description: additional reaction time for Different stimuli relative to Same stimuli

triples.name: incorrect minus correct reaction time
triples.description: additional reaction time for incorrect responses relative to correct responses

triples.name: mean disparity angle
triples.description: mean angle of disparity of the stimulus pairs used

triples.name: self rated group accuracy
triples.description: participants' mean rating of the group's accuracy

triples.name: self rated computer accuracy
triples.description: participants' mean rating of the computers' accuracy

triples.name: run trial count
triples.description: number of trials in one run with the group (or computers)

triples.name: conformity trial count
triples.description: mean number of conformity trials per participant

triples.name: field strength
triples.description: magnetic field strength of the MRI scanner in tesla

triples.name: smoothing kernel
triples.description: full width of the isotropic gaussian smoothing kernel in mm

triples.name: significance threshold
triples.description: uncorrected p-value threshold used for selecting activated voxels

triples.name: minimum cluster size
triples.description: minimum number of contiguous voxels required for a significant cluster

triples.name: conformity error increase
triples.description: absolute increase in error rate from baseline to the group-incorrect condition

triples.name: social conformity error factor
triples.description: ratio of group-incorrect error rate to computer-incorrect error rate; isolates the human social component

triples.name: intraparietal sulcus cluster size
triples.description: size in voxels of the right intraparietal sulcus cluster for Group > Computer information

triples.name: intraparietal sulcus t statistic
triples.description: t statistic of the right intraparietal sulcus cluster for Group > Computer information

triples.name: conformity ROI mean difference
triples.description: mean BOLD beta difference for conforming to the Group versus the Computers in the right intraparietal sulcus ROI

triples.name: amygdala activation
triples.description: right amygdala activation associated with social independence

triples.name: caudate activation
triples.description: right caudate head activation associated with social independence

social conformity study 2005 Berns

formulas.name: social conformity error factor formula
formulas.expression: "social conformity error factor" = "group incorrect error rate" / "computer incorrect error rate"

formulas.name: conformity error increase formula
formulas.expression: "conformity error increase" = "group incorrect error rate" - "baseline error rate"

sources.name: Berns paper
sources.description: Berns GS, Chappelow J, Zink CF, Pagnoni G, Martin-Skurski ME, Richards J (2005): Neurobiological Correlates of Social Conformity and Independence During Mental Rotation. Biol Psychiatry 58:245-253
